title: Menu-bar agent, slide-down history overlay & multi-window editor
goal: Re-architect Photonz to be a CleanShot-style resident MENU-BAR AGENT, not a window-first app. The agent always runs (user can Quit it), owns capture + global hotkeys + the updater, and surfaces history as a GLOBAL top-of-screen slide-down overlay. The editor becomes on-demand and MULTI-WINDOW (one window per image; editing a history item opens a new window or focuses the existing one). This replaces phase-9's single-window, in-editor history carousel. Design: docs/design/capture.md. Keep capture/history/updater LOGIC in testable core/store types; the NSStatusItem/NSPanel/window shell stays thin. TDD for PhotonzCore/Render/CaptureStore changes.

tasks.title: App split: resident menu-bar AppCoordinator + per-window EditorState
tasks.notes: ARCHITECTURAL PREREQUISITE. Today a single app-wide AppState backs one EditorView. Split into: (a) AppCoordinator (@MainActor @Observable) — the resident root: status item, HotkeyCenter, CaptureCenter/CaptureStore, updater, window registry; survives with zero windows open. (b) EditorState (rename of today's AppState) — per editor window: its own History/ImageStore/DocumentRenderer/document. Run as a menu-bar agent (LSUIElement or setActivationPolicy(.accessory)); closing the last editor window must NOT quit — only the menu's Quit does. DECIDED: editor windows = SwiftUI WindowGroup(for: CaptureID.self) (value-based; reuse gives focus-existing for free — see 11.5), plus a file-backed variant for opened images. Verify global hotkeys fire with no key window. See capture.md 'Process & window topology'.

tasks.title: Status-item menu (NSStatusItem)
tasks.notes: Menu: Capture Region (⌘⇧4), Capture Full Screen (⌘⇧3), Record Screen/Video… (wired in phase 12; item present now), Open History (⌘⇧H), divider, Check for Updates… (11.6), Preferences… (later/stub), About Photonz, Quit Photonz. Menu set is open-ended. Icon = template image. All actions work from the resident agent with no editor window open.

tasks.title: System shortcut overrides ⌘⇧3 (full) / ⌘⇧4 (region) from the agent
tasks.notes: Refine phase-9 Carbon hotkeys so ⌘⇧3 = full screen, ⌘⇧4 = region, captured into CaptureStore from the resident agent (no window needed). Handle the Screen Recording permission flow (macOS 26 TCC caveats — see progress log) and the conflict with macOS's own Screenshot shortcuts (guide the user to disable the system bindings, or document it). Keep menu items working regardless of hotkey state.

tasks.title: Global slide-down history overlay (⌘⇧H) — replaces the in-editor carousel
tasks.notes: REPLACES phase-9 HistoryPanel + capture.isHistoryVisible inside EditorView (remove them). A borderless, non-activating NSPanel pinned to the TOP edge of the active display, Liquid Glass styling, above normal windows. ANIMATION: show = slide DOWN from the top edge + fade IN; dismiss = slide UP + fade OUT (one spring; remove panel on completion). Dismiss on Esc / click-away / re-press ⌘⇧H / action chosen. Contents: newest-first thumbnails (capped by CaptureHistory); per item Copy, Edit (→ 11.5), drag-file-out, Delete. CaptureStore/CaptureHistory reused unchanged. Overlay placement geometry is testable.

tasks.title: Multi-window editor + edit round-trip (open-new vs focus-existing)
tasks.notes: Edit on a history item (or Quick Access Overlay) opens that capture in an EDITOR WINDOW via openWindow(value: captureID) on the WindowGroup(for: CaptureID.self) (DECIDED in 11.1): SwiftUI reuses the existing window for that id, giving FOCUS-EXISTING for free; a new id opens a fresh window. Multiple editor windows edit different images independently. ROUND-TRIP (was 11.4): on save/close of an edited capture, prompt Override (replace the history entry in place) vs Save as new (derived entry). CaptureStore replace/derive is TDD; the prompt is app-side. Also support opening arbitrary image/.photonz files in their own editor windows.

tasks.title: Check for Updates
tasks.notes: Menu 'Check for Updates…' compares the running VERSION against the published site/version.json (release pipeline keeps it in lockstep — see release.md) and, if newer, offers to download the DMG from the GitHub release. DECIDED: lightweight CUSTOM version.json check (no Sparkle); version-compare logic is a testable core type, fetch/UI is the thin shell. Surface 'you're up to date' / 'update available' clearly.

tasks.title: Quick Access Overlay (post-capture floating thumbnail)
tasks.notes: CLEANSHOT SIGNATURE FEATURE (competitive-cleanshot.md): after any capture, show a small floating thumbnail in a screen corner with quick actions — Copy, Save, Edit (→ opens/focuses an editor window via 11.5), drag-and-drop the file out, Delete — and a configurable auto-close timeout. Must feel instant and get out of the way. Borderless always-on-top NSPanel; reuses CaptureStore + the edit round-trip.

tasks.title: Pin-to-screen: float a capture above all windows
tasks.notes: CLEANSHOT SIGNATURE FEATURE: pin a screenshot as a floating, always-on-top window kept visible while you work (reference material), adjustable size + opacity, draggable anywhere. Borderless floating NSWindow; invokable from the Quick Access Overlay (11.7) or the history overlay (11.4).
